# Findings and Recommendations (v2)

**Project:** B&R Capital Dashboard Interface
**Generated:** 2026-03-10
**Generated by:** Team 66 (Regeneration) -- Validation, Index & Findings Consolidation
**Source documents:** 9 v2 architecture/documentation files produced by regeneration teams + agent-team-reference.md
**Previous version:** v1 (2026-03-09) had 107 findings; 51 improvement tasks have been completed since then.

---

## Summary

| Severity                       | Open Count                          |
| ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------- |
| CRITICAL                       | 7 (+ 1 resolved)                    |
| HIGH                           | 15                                  |

---

## Critical Findings

### F-001: Users Endpoints Backed by In-Memory Demo Data, Not Database

- **Severity:** CRITICAL
- **Source:** Team 62 -- backend-api-reference.md (Issue 1)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/users.py`
- **Description:** All user endpoints operate on a module-level `DEMO_USERS` list. The database session is injected but never used. `POST /users/`, `PUT /users/{id}`, and `DELETE /users/{id}` mutate only the in-memory list -- changes are lost on process restart.
- **Impact:** User creation, updates, and deactivation do not persist. Critical for any production use.
- **Recommended Fix:** Replace in-memory list with `crud.user` database operations using the injected `AsyncSession`.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 12 (API Endpoint Scaffold) -- Backend Architect + Test Engineer

---

### F-002: Monitoring Liveness/Readiness Probes Guarded by `require_admin`

- **Severity:** CRITICAL
- **Source:** Team 62 -- backend-api-reference.md (Issue 13)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/monitoring.py` (line 25)
- **Description:** `router = APIRouter(dependencies=[Depends(require_admin)])` applies admin authentication to all monitoring routes, including `/health/live` and `/health/ready`. Kubernetes liveness and readiness probes cannot authenticate with a Bearer token and will receive 401, causing pods to be continuously marked unhealthy.
- **Impact:** Blocks production Kubernetes deployment entirely.
- **Recommended Fix:** Exempt `/health/live` and `/health/ready` from the router-level auth dependency by defining them on a separate unprotected router or overriding the dependency on those specific routes.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 12 (API Endpoint Scaffold)

---

### F-003: Report Generation Has No Background Worker

- **Severity:** CRITICAL
- **Source:** Team 63 -- data-flow-reference.md (Issue 3)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/reporting.py`
- **Description:** `POST /reporting/generate` creates a `queued_reports` row with status="pending", but no background worker or task runner processes the queue. Reports remain in "pending" status indefinitely. The frontend poll loop will never see status="completed".
- **Impact:** Report generation is non-functional. Users can queue reports but they never complete.
- **Recommended Fix:** Implement a Celery task or background worker that processes the report queue, generates output (PDF/Excel), and updates status to "completed" with a download URL.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 11 (Full-Stack Feature Build)

---

### F-004: WebSocket Token Validation Does Not Check Blacklist

- **Severity:** CRITICAL
- **Source:** Team 62 -- backend-api-reference.md (Issue 14)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/ws.py` (lines 24-43)
- **Description:** `_authenticate_token()` decodes the JWT and extracts `user_id`, but does not call `token_blacklist.is_blacklisted(jti)`. A user who has logged out (token blacklisted) can still establish WebSocket connections using the revoked access token until natural 30-minute expiry.
- **Impact:** Security gap -- revoked tokens remain valid for WebSocket connections.
- **Recommended Fix:** Add `token_blacklist.is_blacklisted(jti)` check to `_authenticate_token()`.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 6 (Security Audit)

---

### F-005: Token Refresh Not Wired on Frontend

- **Severity:** CRITICAL
- **Source:** Team 63 -- data-flow-reference.md (Issue 1); Team 64 -- architecture-decisions.md (Issue 4.9)
- **Location:** `src/stores/authStore.ts`, `src/lib/api/client.ts`
- **Description:** The backend implements full refresh token rotation with replay detection (`POST /auth/refresh`), but the frontend does not call this endpoint. On 401, the frontend clears state and forces re-login. The `refresh_token` stored in localStorage is set but never used. Previously identified in v1; remains unresolved.
- **Impact:** Users must re-login when the 30-minute access token expires instead of seamless refresh. Short effective session lifetime.
- **Recommended Fix:** Implement a retry-after-refresh mechanism in the fetch client's `request()` function. On 401, attempt `POST /auth/refresh` with the stored refresh token. If successful, retry the original request. If refresh fails, then force logout.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 11 (Full-Stack Feature Build)

---

### F-006: Financial Calculation Libraries Have No Tests

- **Severity:** CRITICAL
- **Source:** Team 65 -- test-coverage.md (Gap C1)
- **Location:** `src/lib/calculations/irr.ts`, `cashflow.ts`, `sensitivity.ts`
- **Description:** Frontend financial calculation libraries (IRR, equity multiple, cash flow projections, sensitivity analysis) have zero test coverage. CLAUDE.md mandates tests for all financial calculations. These drive investment decisions.
- **Impact:** Incorrect financial calculations could lead to wrong investment decisions. No regression safety net.
- **Recommended Fix:** Write comprehensive unit tests covering edge cases (negative cash flows, zero IRR, boundary conditions).
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 4 (Test Gap Analysis) -- Explorer + Test Engineer

---

### F-007a: Application Startup Crash — `structlog.get_level_from_name` Does Not Exist (RESOLVED)

- **Severity:** CRITICAL
- **Source:** Runtime testing (`npm run dev:all`)
- **Location:** `backend/app/core/logging.py`, line 74
- **Description:** `setup_structlog()` called `structlog.get_level_from_name(settings.LOG_LEVEL.lower())`, but this function does not exist in structlog 25.5.0. The application crashes on startup with `AttributeError: module structlog has no attribute get_level_from_name`. This was introduced in Task #20 (structured logging) and was not caught because backend tests mock/bypass the lifespan startup.
- **Impact:** Backend server cannot start. Complete application outage.
- **Resolution:** Replaced with `logging.getLevelName(settings.LOG_LEVEL.upper())` (stdlib). `make_filtering_bound_logger()` accepts a stdlib int level. Fix verified — server starts cleanly, 2,141 tests pass.
- **Status:** **RESOLVED** (2026-03-10)

---

### F-007: Transaction DELETE/Restore Has No Auth Upgrade Over `require_viewer`

- **Severity:** CRITICAL
- **Source:** Team 62 -- backend-api-reference.md (Issue 10)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/transactions.py`
- **Description:** `delete_transaction` and `restore_transaction` are protected only by router-level `require_viewer`. Any authenticated user (including viewers) can soft-delete and restore financial transactions. Deals require `require_manager` for equivalent operations.
- **Impact:** Authorization bypass -- viewers should not be able to delete or restore financial records.
- **Recommended Fix:** Add `require_manager` dependency to `delete_transaction` and `restore_transaction` endpoints.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 6 (Security Audit)

---

## High Findings

### F-008: Document Route Shadowing (`/property/{id}` vs `/{document_id}`)

- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Source:** Team 62 -- backend-api-reference.md (Issue 11)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/documents.py` (line 394)
- **Description:** Route `/property/{property_id}` is registered after `/{document_id}`. FastAPI matches `/{document_id}` first for requests to `/documents/property/123` because "property" satisfies `{document_id}`. Results in 404.
- **Impact:** Document-by-property lookup is broken at the routing level.
- **Recommended Fix:** Move the `/property/{property_id}` route definition before `/{document_id}`, or rename to use a prefix like `/by-property/{property_id}`.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 12 (API Endpoint Scaffold)

---

### F-009: Document Upload Does Not Require Elevated Role

- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Source:** Team 62 -- backend-api-reference.md (Issue 12)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/documents.py`
- **Description:** `POST /documents/upload` inherits only `require_viewer` from the router. Any authenticated viewer can upload documents (up to 50 MB Excel files).
- **Impact:** Unauthorized file uploads by low-privilege users.
- **Recommended Fix:** Require at minimum `require_analyst` for document uploads.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 6 (Security Audit)

---

### F-010: Proforma Returns Matches by `property_name` String, Not `property_id`

- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Source:** Team 62 -- backend-api-reference.md (Issue 5)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/deals.py` (line ~1562)
- **Description:** `GET /deals/{deal_id}/proforma-returns` queries `ExtractedValue.property_name` against the deal name. If names don't exactly match (even after regex stripping), no data is returned. A join through `property_id` would be more reliable.
- **Impact:** Proforma data silently returns empty for deals with slightly mismatched names.
- **Recommended Fix:** Join through `property_id` foreign key rather than string matching on names.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 9 (Database Query Review)

---

### F-011: PUT and PATCH on Deals Have Identical Behavior

- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Source:** Team 62 -- backend-api-reference.md (Issue 4)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/deals.py`
- **Description:** Both `update_deal` (PUT) and `patch_deal` (PATCH) accept `DealUpdate` (all fields optional) and both use `model_dump(exclude_unset=True)`. PUT behaves as a partial update, not a full replacement. Clients expecting PUT to null out omitted fields will encounter unexpected behavior.
- **Impact:** API contract violation -- REST semantics expect PUT to replace the entire resource.
- **Recommended Fix:** Either make PUT accept a full `DealCreate`-like schema requiring all fields, or document that PUT is treated as PATCH and deprecate one endpoint.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 7 (API Contract Validation)

---

### F-012: 304 Response Without Cache Hit Falls Through Silently

- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Source:** Team 61 -- frontend-components.md (Issue 2)
- **Location:** `src/lib/api/client.ts:80-86`
- **Description:** If a 304 response is received but `etagCache.get(url)` returns `undefined` (e.g., cache was cleared between request and response), the function continues to `!response.ok`. Since 304 is not `ok`, it throws an `ApiError`.
- **Impact:** Intermittent errors during normal operation if ETag cache is cleared (e.g., on logout).
- **Recommended Fix:** Add explicit handling for 304 without cache: retry without `If-None-Match` header, or return a descriptive error.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 11 (Full-Stack Feature Build)

---

### F-013: `user_name` Always `None` in PropertyActivityResponse

- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Source:** Team 62 -- backend-api-reference.md (Issue 7)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/properties.py` (line 727)
- **Description:** `user_name=None` is hardcoded with comment "Would need join with users table." Clients display null for all activity authors.
- **Impact:** Activity feed shows no attribution -- users cannot see who performed actions.
- **Recommended Fix:** Add a JOIN to the users table in the activity query, or use a subquery to resolve `user_id` to display name.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 9 (Database Query Review)

---

### F-014: Analytics Export Uses Static Mock Data

- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Source:** Team 62 -- backend-api-reference.md (Issue 9)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/exports.py` (lines 183-211)
- **Description:** `GET /api/v1/exports/analytics/excel` uses a hardcoded `dashboard_metrics` dict (45 properties, $425M value, etc.) instead of querying the live database.
- **Impact:** Exported analytics reports contain fabricated data, not actual portfolio metrics.
- **Recommended Fix:** Replace static dict with actual aggregation queries against the properties and deals tables.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 11 (Full-Stack Feature Build)

---

### F-015: Growth Rate Fields Always Return `0.0` or `null`

- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Source:** Team 62 -- backend-api-reference.md (Issue 8)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/analytics.py`, `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/properties.py`
- **Description:** `ytd_noi_growth`, `ytd_rent_growth`, `total_return`, `income_return`, `appreciation_return`, `benchmark_return`, and `cycle_times_days` are hardcoded to `0.0` or `None`.
- **Impact:** Analytics dashboard shows zero growth across all metrics. Users get misleading data.
- **Recommended Fix:** Implement time-series tracking for these metrics, or clearly mark them as "not yet available" in the UI rather than showing 0.0.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 11 (Full-Stack Feature Build)

---

### F-016: Redis Services Commented Out in Lifespan Startup

- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Source:** Team 63 -- data-flow-reference.md (Issue 2)
- **Location:** `backend/app/main.py` (lines 189-191)
- **Description:** Redis initialization and WebSocket manager initialization are commented out. Cache and rate-limit state fall back to in-memory storage, which is not shared across workers.
- **Impact:** In production with multiple workers (WORKERS=4), each worker maintains independent rate-limit counters and cache -- a user could exceed rate limits by 4x, and cache invalidation on one worker doesn't affect others.
- **Recommended Fix:** Uncomment Redis initialization in lifespan, or implement worker-aware initialization that connects to Redis when available.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 20 (Environment Config Audit)

---

### F-017: Backend Coverage Below 30% Threshold

- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Source:** Team 65 -- test-coverage.md (Section 4.7)
- **Location:** Backend test suite (26.20% coverage)
- **Description:** Backend line coverage is 26.20%, below the configured 30% threshold. The gap is driven by large untested service modules (workflow engine, schedulers, construction API integrations).
- **Impact:** CI coverage gate fails. Large swathes of production code have no regression safety net.
- **Recommended Fix:** Add tests for `cache.py`, `interest_rates.py`, and key CRUD files to push past the 30% threshold. See test-coverage.md Section 7.6 for prioritized recommendations.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 4 (Test Gap Analysis) -- Explorer + Test Engineer

---

### F-018: Alembic Migrations Lag Behind Model Changes

- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Source:** Team 60 -- database-schema.md (Drift Notes, Section 16)
- **Location:** `backend/alembic/versions/` (HEAD: `7c415cc1b77a`)
- **Description:** Multiple model features appear after the latest migration (2026-02-12): `audit_logs_admin` table, SoftDeleteMixin columns, `version` column, `stage_order`, 41 CHECK constraints, composite indexes. These may have been applied via manual DDL or are only in the model but not in the live schema.
- **Impact:** Running `alembic upgrade head` on a fresh database will not create these structures. Schema drift between model and database.
- **Recommended Fix:** Generate a new Alembic migration that captures all model changes since `7c415cc1b77a`.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 24 (Migration Consolidation)

---

### F-019: N+1 Query in Queued Report List

- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Source:** Team 63 -- data-flow-reference.md (Issue 4)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/reporting.py` (lines 246-266)
- **Description:** Each queued report triggers a separate `template_crud.get(db, item.template_id)` inside a for-loop. For N queued reports, this issues N+1 queries.
- **Impact:** Slow response for large report queues.
- **Recommended Fix:** Single JOIN query or batch template fetch before the loop.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 9 (Database Query Review)

---

### F-020: `useDeals` Hardcoded `page_size: 100` Truncates Results

- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Source:** Team 61 -- frontend-components.md (Issue 12)
- **Location:** `src/hooks/api/useDeals.ts:66`
- **Description:** `useDealsWithMockFallback` fetches with `page_size: 100` hardcoded. Portfolios with more than 100 deals are silently truncated.
- **Impact:** Deals beyond the first 100 are invisible to users on the kanban board and deal list.
- **Recommended Fix:** Accept `pageSize` as parameter, implement pagination or infinite scroll.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 11 (Full-Stack Feature Build)

---

### F-021: `POST /properties/{id}/activities` Returns 200, Not 201

- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Source:** Team 62 -- backend-api-reference.md (Issue 6)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/properties.py` (line 748)
- **Description:** Create endpoint does not set `status_code=201`, returning 200 instead. Inconsistent with all other create endpoints.
- **Impact:** API contract inconsistency. Clients checking for 201 on resource creation will misinterpret.
- **Recommended Fix:** Add `status_code=201` to the route decorator.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 7 (API Contract Validation)

---

### F-022: Architecture Doc Says `POST /deals/compare`; Implementation Is `GET`

- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Source:** Team 62 -- backend-api-reference.md (Issue 2)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/deals.py` (~line 506); `docs/backend-architecture.md`
- **Description:** Documentation incorrectly lists deal comparison as POST; actual implementation is GET with query params.
- **Impact:** Developers relying on docs will send incorrect requests.
- **Recommended Fix:** Update backend-architecture.md to reflect `GET /api/v1/deals/compare?ids=1,2,3`.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 15 (API Documentation Generation)

---

## Medium Findings

### F-023: ETag Cache Unbounded (Frontend)

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 61 -- frontend-components.md (Issue 1); Team 63 -- data-flow-reference.md (Issue 7); Team 64 -- architecture-decisions.md (Issue 4.8)
- **Location:** `src/lib/api/client.ts:14`
- **Description:** The `etagCache` Map grows without bound as the user navigates. No LRU eviction or max-size limit. Multiple v2 teams independently flagged this.
- **Impact:** Memory growth in long-running sessions, especially for power users viewing many property details.
- **Recommended Fix:** Add LRU eviction (cap at 200-500 entries) or clear on logout.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 11 (Full-Stack Feature Build)

---

### F-024: WebSocket Auth Token Not Updated on Refresh

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 61 -- frontend-components.md (Issue 4)
- **Location:** `src/hooks/useWebSocket.ts:119`
- **Description:** Auth token is read from `authStore.getState().accessToken` at WebSocket connect time. If the token refreshes while connected, the connection continues with the old token.
- **Impact:** Long-lived WebSocket connections may become unauthenticated after token refresh.
- **Recommended Fix:** Add re-authentication mechanism or reconnect when token changes.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 11 (Full-Stack Feature Build)

---

### F-025: Filter Persistence Potential Infinite Loop

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 61 -- frontend-components.md (Issue 6)
- **Location:** `src/hooks/useFilterPersistence.ts`
- **Description:** Bidirectional sync between URL params and filter state has potential for infinite update loops if source-of-truth ref tracking has edge cases during rapid navigation + filter changes.
- **Impact:** Page freezes in edge cases.
- **Recommended Fix:** Add a guard counter or stabilize with `useRef` debounce to break potential cycles.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 23 (State Management Cleanup)

---

### F-026: In-Memory Cache Fallback Has No Automatic Cleanup

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 64 -- architecture-decisions.md (Issue 4.2)
- **Location:** `backend/app/core/cache.py`
- **Description:** `CacheService._memory_cache` stores entries with TTL-based expiration, but expired entries are only evicted on read or explicit `cleanup_memory()` call. No background task calls `cleanup_memory()`.
- **Impact:** In long-running development sessions without Redis, expired entries accumulate in memory.
- **Recommended Fix:** Add periodic cleanup to the lifespan manager or use a TTL-aware data structure.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 20 (Environment Config Audit)

---

### F-027: WebSocket ConnectionManager Not Integrated into Lifespan

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 64 -- architecture-decisions.md (Issue 4.3)
- **Location:** `backend/app/services/websocket_manager.py`, `backend/app/main.py`
- **Description:** The ConnectionManager singleton is created lazily but `main.py` has its initialization commented out. No graceful shutdown disconnects active connections on application exit.
- **Impact:** WebSocket connections may be dropped without cleanup on shutdown.
- **Recommended Fix:** Uncomment WebSocket init in lifespan and add `connection_manager.disconnect_all()` to shutdown.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 20 (Environment Config Audit)

---

### F-028: Rate Limiter Redis Backend Uses Fixed-Bucket Approximation

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 64 -- architecture-decisions.md (Issue 4.7)
- **Location:** `backend/app/middleware/rate_limiter.py`
- **Description:** Redis backend uses fixed-bucket counter instead of true sliding window. Burst at window boundary could allow 2x the configured limit. Memory backend uses true sliding window -- behavior diverges between backends.
- **Impact:** Rate limiting is less accurate with Redis than with memory backend.
- **Recommended Fix:** Implement sliding window in Redis using sorted sets, or document the approximation.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 6 (Security Audit)

---

### F-029: Fetch Client Missing Token Refresh Logic

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 64 -- architecture-decisions.md (Issue 4.9)
- **Location:** `src/lib/api/client.ts`
- **Description:** The unified fetch client dispatches `auth:unauthorized` on 401, forcing logout. No transparent token refresh. The old axios client had interceptor-based refresh; this was not ported during migration. (Overlaps with F-005 but from the client-layer perspective.)
- **Impact:** Same as F-005 -- forced re-login on token expiry.
- **Recommended Fix:** See F-005.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 11 (Full-Stack Feature Build)

---

### F-030: Enrichment Logic Mixed into CRUD Layer

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 64 -- architecture-decisions.md (Issue 4.10)
- **Location:** `backend/app/crud/crud_property.py`
- **Description:** 260+ lines of field-mapping and JSON-building logic in `enrich_financial_data()`. Mixes data access (CRUD) with business logic (field name mapping, unit conversion). Field mapping constants are extraction-domain concepts in a CRUD file.
- **Impact:** Reduces maintainability. Changes to extraction fields require modifying the CRUD layer.
- **Recommended Fix:** Extract field mapping and JSON building into `services/enrichment.py`. Move field mapping constants to a shared location.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 21 (Large File Decomposition)

---

### F-031: Property Analytics Trends Returns Single-Point Data

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 63 -- data-flow-reference.md (Issue 6)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/properties.py` (lines 630-636)
- **Description:** `GET /properties/{id}/analytics` returns `trends.rent = [current_rent]` and `trends.periods = ["Current"]`. Frontend chart components expect multi-point arrays.
- **Impact:** Analytics charts render a single dot instead of a trend line.
- **Recommended Fix:** Implement time-series tracking, or clearly indicate single-point data in the response so frontend can adapt rendering.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 11 (Full-Stack Feature Build)

---

### F-032: N+1 in Distribution Schedule List

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 63 -- data-flow-reference.md (Issue 5)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/reporting.py` (lines 335-362)
- **Description:** `list_schedules()` queries the template per schedule inside a loop. Same N+1 pattern as F-019.
- **Impact:** Minor (fewer schedules than reports typically), but inconsistent with batch patterns used elsewhere.
- **Recommended Fix:** Batch template fetch.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 9 (Database Query Review)

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 65 -- test-coverage.md (Gap C4)
- **Location:** `backend/app/core/cache.py`
- **Description:** Redis/memory fallback, TTL expiration, invalidation patterns are all untested.
- **Impact:** Cache bugs could serve stale financial data. No regression safety.
- **Recommended Fix:** Write unit tests covering Redis mode, memory fallback, TTL expiry, and pattern invalidation.

### F-038: `authStore` Has No Tests

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 65 -- test-coverage.md (Gap C3)
- **Location:** `src/stores/authStore.ts`
- **Description:** Login, logout, initialize, and 401 event listener are untested. This is the core authentication state for the entire frontend.

### F-039: ETag Middleware Has No Tests

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 65 -- test-coverage.md (Section 4.6)
- **Location:** `backend/app/middleware/etag.py`
- **Description:** ETag generation, 304 responses, and cache-key correctness are untested.
- **Impact:** Wrong ETags could cause stale data or cache misses.
- **Recommended Fix:** Add middleware tests verifying ETag generation and 304 behavior.

### F-040: Origin Validation Middleware Has No Tests

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 65 -- test-coverage.md (Section 4.6)
- **Location:** `backend/app/main.py` (inline middleware)
- **Description:** CSRF defense-in-depth middleware is untested.
- **Recommended Fix:** Add tests verifying origin/referer validation on mutating requests.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 4 (Test Gap Analysis)

---

### F-041: `documents.property_id` Is VARCHAR(50), Not FK to `properties.id`

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 60 -- database-schema.md (Drift Note 1)
- **Location:** `backend/app/models/document.py`
- **Description:** `documents.property_id` is VARCHAR(50) rather than an INTEGER foreign key to `properties.id`. This prevents referential integrity enforcement and JOIN optimization.
- **Impact:** Orphaned document records possible. Query performance degraded for document-by-property lookups.
- **Recommended Fix:** Investigate whether this is intentional (external IDs) or a schema error. If unintentional, create an Alembic migration to change the column type and add FK constraint.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 24 (Migration Consolidation)

---

### F-042: Construction Pipeline Uses VARCHAR Without DB-Level Constraints

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 60 -- database-schema.md (Drift Notes 3-4)
- **Location:** `backend/app/models/construction.py`, `backend/app/models/transaction.py`, `backend/app/models/document.py`
- **Description:** `pipeline_status`, `primary_classification`, `transactions.type`, and `documents.type` use plain VARCHAR despite having Python StrEnum definitions. No CHECK constraints enforce valid values at the database level.
- **Impact:** Invalid enum values can be inserted directly via SQL or data imports.
- **Recommended Fix:** Add CHECK constraints or use PostgreSQL ENUM types via Alembic migration.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 24 (Migration Consolidation)

---

### F-043: Zod `common.ts` and `construction.ts` Schemas Have No Tests

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 65 -- test-coverage.md (Gaps M7, M8)
- **Location:** `src/lib/api/schemas/common.ts`, `src/lib/api/schemas/construction.ts`
- **Description:** `dateString` and `numericString` transforms in `common.ts` are used by all other schemas but have no dedicated tests. 12 construction schemas also untested.
- **Recommended Fix:** Add tests for transform edge cases (null, undefined, malformed dates/numbers).
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 4 (Test Gap Analysis)

---

### F-044: Deal Kanban Enrichment Runs on Every Cache Miss

- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Source:** Team 63 -- data-flow-reference.md (Issue 8)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/deals.py` (`_enrich_deals_with_extraction`)
- **Description:** When 5-minute kanban cache expires, the entire enrichment subquery runs across all properties. For 305 properties, this is a substantial query.
- **Impact:** Frequent expensive queries under normal usage patterns.
- **Recommended Fix:** Consider longer cache TTL or materialized view for extraction enrichment data.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 8 (Performance Profiling)

---

## Low Findings

### F-045: ADR-004 Is Stale (Dual Client Pattern)

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 64 -- architecture-decisions.md (Issue 4.1)
- **Location:** `docs/adr/004-dual-api-client-pattern.md`
- **Description:** Documents the axios/fetch dual client pattern. Axios has been fully removed. ADR should be marked Superseded.
- **Recommended Fix:** Mark ADR-004 as Superseded, create ADR-008 for unified fetch client.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 15 (API Documentation Generation)

---

### F-046: Dual Logging Imports Require Discipline

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 64 -- architecture-decisions.md (Issue 4.4)
- **Location:** Multiple backend files
- **Description:** Both `loguru.logger` and `structlog.get_logger()` are valid per ADR-006, but some modules use both. Convention (loguru for general, structlog for audit) is not enforced by linting.
- **Recommended Fix:** Consider a ruff rule or code review checklist item.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 5 (Lint & Style Enforcement)

---

### F-047: Non-JSON Responses Return `{} as T`

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 61 -- frontend-components.md (Issue 3)
- **Location:** `src/lib/api/client.ts:120`
- **Description:** Non-JSON successful responses return `{} as T` -- a type assertion that silently produces incorrect type at runtime.
- **Recommended Fix:** Return `undefined` or `null` and update `T` constraint.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 3 (Type Coverage Sweep)

---

### F-048: WebSocket `callbacksRef` Updated Every Render

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 61 -- frontend-components.md (Issue 5)
- **Location:** `src/hooks/useWebSocket.ts:108-110`
- **Description:** `callbacksRef` updated in effect without dependency array, triggering ESLint warnings. Intentional but noisy.
- **Recommended Fix:** Add `eslint-disable-next-line` comment or use layout effect.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 5 (Lint & Style Enforcement)

---

### F-049: VirtualizedTable Spacer Missing `colSpan`

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 61 -- frontend-components.md (Issue 7)
- **Location:** `src/components/shared/VirtualizedTable.tsx:169`
- **Description:** Spacer `<td>` elements do not set `colSpan` to match actual column count. Causes visual misalignment in multi-column tables.
- **Recommended Fix:** Accept `columnCount` prop or infer from header, set `colSpan`.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 13 (Component Library Extraction)

---

### F-050: Error Tracking Rate Limit Uses Fixed Window

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 61 -- frontend-components.md (Issue 8)
- **Location:** `src/services/errorTracking.ts:42-45`
- **Description:** 60s timeout from first error means all remaining errors in next 59s are dropped if 10 occur in the first second.
- **Recommended Fix:** Consider sliding window (ring buffer of timestamps).
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 22 (Error Handling Standardization)

---

### F-051: Toast Auto-Removal Timer Not Cleared on Manual Dismiss

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 61 -- frontend-components.md (Issue 9)
- **Location:** `src/stores/notificationStore.ts:34`
- **Description:** `setTimeout` not cleared if toast manually dismissed. Idempotent but wastes timer resources.
- **Recommended Fix:** Store timer IDs in a Map and `clearTimeout` on manual removal.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 23 (State Management Cleanup)

---

### F-052: `authStore` Event Listener Registered at Module Load

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 61 -- frontend-components.md (Issue 10)
- **Location:** `src/stores/authStore.ts:100`
- **Description:** `auth:unauthorized` event listener registered as side effect of module import. Would throw in SSR/test environments without `window`. Currently safe (SPA-only).
- **Recommended Fix:** Guard with `typeof window !== 'undefined'` check for future-proofing.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 3 (Type Coverage Sweep)

---

### F-053: Duplicate `formatDate` Implementations

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 61 -- frontend-components.md (Issue 11)
- **Location:** `src/lib/dateUtils.ts` and various components
- **Description:** Components may have local `formatDate` implementations not yet migrated to the shared `dateUtils.ts`.
- **Recommended Fix:** Audit all components for local date formatting and migrate to `dateUtils.ts`.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 2 (Dead Code / Unused Import Cleanup)

---

### F-054: Report Queue 30-Second Polling

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 61 -- frontend-components.md (Issue 13)
- **Location:** `src/hooks/api/useReporting.ts`
- **Description:** Report queue uses 30-second `staleTime` for frequent refetches. Multiple users on reporting page create significant server load.
- **Recommended Fix:** Consider WebSocket-based updates for report queue status.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 8 (Performance Profiling)

---

### F-055: Comparison Deals Not Persisted to Storage

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 61 -- frontend-components.md (Issue 14)
- **Location:** `src/contexts/QuickActionsContext.tsx`
- **Description:** Watchlist is persisted to localStorage but comparison selection is lost on page refresh.
- **Recommended Fix:** Persist comparison deals to localStorage or sessionStorage.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 23 (State Management Cleanup)

---

### F-056: Optimistic Locking Only on Deal Model

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 64 -- architecture-decisions.md (Issue 4.5)
- **Location:** `backend/app/models/deal.py`
- **Description:** Only `Deal` has version column. `Property` edits from multiple users could encounter lost-update problems as the app grows.
- **Recommended Fix:** Monitor for concurrent-edit scenarios on Property; add version column if needed.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 9 (Database Query Review)

---

### F-057: SoftDeleteMixin Scope May Need Expansion

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 64 -- architecture-decisions.md (Issue 4.6)
- **Location:** Backend models
- **Description:** Activity logs, extracted values, and report templates are hard-deleted. For audit trail completeness, consider soft-delete on `ActivityLog` and `ExtractedValue`.
- **Recommended Fix:** Evaluate compliance requirements; add SoftDeleteMixin if warranted.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 6 (Security Audit)

---

### F-058: Multiple API Hooks Have Zero Test Coverage

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 65 -- test-coverage.md (Sections 5.3-5.5)
- **Location:** `src/hooks/api/useDeals.ts` (11 hooks), `src/hooks/api/useProperties.ts` (7 hooks), `src/hooks/api/useTransactions.ts` (8 hooks), and 5 other hook modules
- **Description:** Core API hooks for deals, properties, transactions, documents, interest rates, market data, and reporting have no dedicated tests.
- **Recommended Fix:** Prioritize `useDeals` and `useProperties` hooks; add tests for query configuration, error handling, and cache invalidation.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 4 (Test Gap Analysis)

- **Description:** `UnderwritingModel`, `AnnualCashflow`, `BudgetAssumptions`, `EquityReturns`, and 9 other underwriting models have no model tests.

### F-063: Feature Components Have Extensive Test Gaps

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 65 -- test-coverage.md (Sections 5.6)
- **Location:** Multiple feature directories (Deals, Properties, Analytics, Market, Documents, Interest Rates, Reporting, Underwriting, Transactions, Auth, Mapping)
- **Description:** Dozens of feature components have zero test coverage. Most are UI-heavy with minimal logic (charts, cards, filters, modals).
- **Impact:** Low individually -- test the logic hooks, not the presentational wrappers.
- **Recommended Fix:** Prioritize testing hooks and data transformation over rendering tests. See test-coverage.md Section 5.6 for complete list.

### F-064: `useUnderwriting` Hook Has No Tests

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 65 -- test-coverage.md (Gap C2)
- **Location:** `src/features/underwriting/hooks/useUnderwriting.ts`
- **Description:** Full underwriting engine computing IRR, sensitivity analysis has no tests. CLAUDE.md mandates tests for financial calculations.
- **Impact:** Wrong underwriting output could mislead investment decisions. Partially mitigated if F-006 (calculation library tests) is resolved.
- **Recommended Fix:** Test the hook's computation logic with known input/output pairs.

### F-067: `PUT /deals/{id}` and `PATCH /deals/{id}` Require `require_manager`, Not `require_analyst`

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 62 -- backend-api-reference.md (Issue 3)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/deals.py` (lines 793, 869)
- **Description:** Architecture reference lists these as `analyst` auth; actual code uses `require_manager`. Documentation inaccuracy.
- **Recommended Fix:** Update architecture docs to reflect correct auth requirement.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 15 (API Documentation Generation)

### F-068: PDF Export May Have Incomplete Financial Data

- **Severity:** LOW
- **Source:** Team 62 -- backend-api-reference.md (Issue 15)
- **Location:** `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/exports.py`
- **Description:** `GET /api/v1/exports/portfolio/pdf` iterates all properties and deals without calling `enrich_financial_data_batch` first. Financial data in PDF may be incomplete for properties not yet enriched.
- **Recommended Fix:** Call `enrich_financial_data_batch` before export generation.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 11 (Full-Stack Feature Build)

- **Location:** `docs/adr/`
- **Description:** ADR-008 (Unified Fetch Client), ADR-009 (Cursor-Based Pagination), ADR-010 (Redis Caching Strategy), ADR-011 (Structured Error Handling Middleware) have been implemented but lack formal ADR documentation.
- **Recommended Fix:** Create formal ADR files for these four decisions.
- **Suggested Agent Team:** Team 15 (API Documentation Generation)

## Cross-Reference Gaps

The following gaps were identified during cross-referencing of the 9 v2 documents.

| #   | Gap Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Affected Documents                                              |
| --- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| G-1 | **backend-architecture.md lists `POST /deals/compare`; actual implementation is `GET`**. backend-api-reference.md correctly documents it as GET. Architecture doc needs update.                                                                                                   | backend-architecture.md, backend-api-reference.md               |
| G-2 | **backend-architecture.md lists `analyst` auth for deal PUT/PATCH**; actual code and api-reference both show `manager`. Architecture doc needs update.                                                                                                                            | backend-architecture.md, backend-api-reference.md               |
| G-3 | **test-coverage.md reports 3,140 total tests (2,155 backend + 985 frontend)**; MEMORY.md checkpoint reports 2,577 total (1,700 + 877). The v2 audit picked up additional test files added after the MEMORY.md checkpoint. Both numbers are valid for their respective points in time. | test-coverage.md, MEMORY.md                                     |
| G-4 | **database-schema.md documents 15 migrations through `7c415cc1b77a`** but notes model features that post-date this migration. No doc covers how these were applied.                                                                                                                 | database-schema.md                                              |
| G-5 | **frontend-architecture.md and frontend-components.md both document the ETag cache**. frontend-components.md provides more detail (including the 304-without-cache-hit bug). No conflict, but readers may miss the bug report if they only read the architecture doc.                 | frontend-architecture.md, frontend-components.md                |
| G-6 | **data-flow-reference.md documents the fetch client as the single API client**; this is consistent with architecture-decisions.md noting axios removal. ADR-004 in `docs/adr/` has not been updated to reflect this.                                                                | data-flow-reference.md, architecture-decisions.md, docs/adr/004 |

---

## Resolved Since v1

The following categories of findings from the v1 report (107 total) have been resolved by the 51 completed improvement tasks. These are summarized rather than listed individually, as the v1 findings document is available for reference.

### N+1 Query Patterns (Previously F-001, F-002)

Deal enrichment and property dashboard enrichment have been converted to batch queries. The main kanban and property list endpoints now use `_enrich_deals_with_extraction` and `enrich_financial_data_batch` respectively, issuing 2-3 queries total instead of N+1. Previously identified; resolved by Tasks #36, #54.

### Token Refresh UI (Partially Resolved)

Backend refresh token rotation with replay detection was implemented (Task #33). Frontend integration remains incomplete (see F-005 above).

### Error Boundary & Toast Consolidation

Global `ErrorBoundary` component added (`src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx`). Toast notifications consolidated into a single `useNotify` hook and `notificationStore`. Previously identified; resolved by Tasks #22, #31.

### Dead Code & Unused Imports

Axios client (`src/lib/api.ts`) and `axios` npm dependency fully removed. Legacy code cleanup completed. Previously identified; resolved by Tasks #2, #40.

### Security Hardening

- Input sanitization via `make_sanitized_validator()` on all user-facing Pydantic schemas
- Rate limiting middleware with per-path rules
- Origin validation middleware (CSRF defense-in-depth)
- Security headers middleware (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options)
- API key authentication for service-to-service calls
- Request ID correlation across all middleware layers
  Previously identified; resolved by Tasks #6, #28, #41, #42, #43, #44.

### Test Suite Expansion

Test count grew from ~2,577 (v1 baseline) to 3,140 (v2 audit): 2,155 backend + 985 frontend. New test coverage includes:

- Cursor-based pagination (CRUD layer)
- Optimistic locking (Deal model)
- Soft-delete/restore (CRUDBase)
- Financial constraints (41 CHECK constraints)
- Input sanitization edge cases
- Rate limiter middleware
- Error handler middleware
- Extraction output validation (9 rules)
- Proforma expansion (30 fields)
  Previously identified; resolved by Tasks #4, #35, #37, #46-49.

### Structured Logging & Observability

- structlog + loguru coexistence formalized (ADR-006)
- Request ID ContextVar propagated to all log entries
- Slow query detection and Prometheus metrics
  Previously identified; resolved by Tasks #30, #44.

### Soft-Delete & Optimistic Locking

- SoftDeleteMixin applied to Deal, Transaction, Property, User
- Version column on Deal with 409 Conflict handling
- CRUDBase auto-filters soft-deleted records
  Previously identified; resolved by Tasks #36, #38.

### Pagination

- Cursor-based pagination implemented as alternative to offset-based
- Base64-encoded `[sort_value, row_id]` cursors with bidirectional traversal
  Previously identified; resolved by Task #39.

---

## Findings Tracking Table

| #       | Severity | Status | Finding                                                            | Fix                                                        | Git Commit # |
| ------- | -------- | ------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------- | ------------ |
| F-001   | CRITICAL | Done   | Users endpoints backed by in-memory demo data, not database        | Replaced with real DB CRUD operations (CRUDUser)           | 8599ec2      |
| F-002   | CRITICAL | Done   | Monitoring liveness/readiness probes guarded by require_admin      | Per-route auth; probes unauthenticated                     | 8599ec2      |
| F-003   | CRITICAL | Done   | Report generation has no background worker                         | Asyncio report_worker with PDF/Excel gen + lifespan hook   | e8e12ce     |
| F-004   | CRITICAL | Done   | WebSocket token validation does not check blacklist                | Added is_blacklisted() check, fail-closed                  | 8599ec2      |
| F-005   | CRITICAL | Done   | Token refresh not wired on frontend                                | Retry-after-refresh in fetch client + authStore method     | 8599ec2      |
| F-006   | CRITICAL | Done   | Financial calculation libraries have no tests                      | 98 tests across IRR, cashflow, sensitivity                 | 8599ec2      |
| F-007a  | CRITICAL | Done   | Structlog startup crash — get_level_from_name does not exist      | logging.getLevelName() (stdlib)                            | 8599ec2      |
| F-007a+ | CRITICAL | Done   | Structlog add_logger_name crash with PrintLoggerFactory            | Removed incompatible processor                             | 8599ec2      |
| F-007   | CRITICAL | Done   | Transaction DELETE/restore has no auth upgrade over require_viewer | Write ops elevated to require_manager                      | 8599ec2      |
| F-008   | HIGH     | Done   | Document route shadowing (/property/{id} vs /{document_id})        | Moved /property/{id} before /{document_id} in router       | a6b0685      |
| F-009   | HIGH     | Done   | Document upload does not require elevated role                     | Added require_analyst dependency to upload endpoint        | a6b0685      |
| F-010   | HIGH     | Done   | Proforma returns matches by property_name string, not property_id  | Prefer property_id FK join, fallback to name matching      | a6b0685      |
| F-011   | HIGH     | Done   | PUT and PATCH on deals have identical behavior                     | Documented PUT as partial update for backwards compat      | a6b0685      |
| F-012   | HIGH     | Done   | 304 response without cache hit falls through silently              | Retry without If-None-Match on cache miss                  | a6b0685      |
| F-013   | HIGH     | Done   | user_name always None in PropertyActivityResponse                  | Batch user lookup via IN query, populate user_name         | a6b0685      |
| F-014   | HIGH     | Done   | Analytics export uses static mock data                             | Live DB queries for portfolio, KPIs, deal pipeline         | f65dc05     |
| F-015   | HIGH     | Done   | Growth rate fields always return 0.0 or null                       | 0.0→None + real cycle_times from activity_logs             | f65dc05     |
| F-016   | HIGH     | Done   | Redis services commented out in lifespan startup                   | Wired with graceful fallback to in-memory                  | 8599ec2      |
| F-017   | HIGH     | Done   | Backend coverage below 30% threshold                               | Already at 63.21% — threshold met                         | bedb94a     |
| F-018   | HIGH     | Done   | Alembic migrations lag behind model changes                        | Catch-up migration: version col, audit_logs_admin, soft-delete, 21 CHECK constraints, 16 indexes | dfbe156     |
| F-019   | HIGH     | Done   | N+1 query in queued report list                                    | Batch template lookup via IN query for reports + schedules | a6b0685      |
| F-020   | HIGH     | Done   | useDeals hardcoded page_size: 100 truncates results                | Configurable pageSize param (default 500)                  | a6b0685      |
| F-021   | HIGH     | Done   | POST /properties/{id}/activities returns 200, not 201              | Added status_code=201 to endpoint decorator                | a6b0685      |
| F-022   | HIGH     | Done   | Architecture doc says POST /deals/compare; implementation is GET   | Corrected doc to GET /deals/compare                        | a6b0685      |
| F-023   | MEDIUM   | Done   | ETag cache unbounded (frontend)                                    | LRU eviction at 100 entries via etagCacheSet()             | f65dc05     |
| F-024   | MEDIUM   | Done   | WebSocket auth token not updated on refresh                        | Subscribe to authStore, reconnect on token change          | f65dc05     |
| F-025   | MEDIUM   | Done   | Filter persistence potential infinite loop                         | isSyncingToUrlRef guard + params diff comparison           | f65dc05     |
| F-026   | MEDIUM   | Done   | In-memory cache fallback has no automatic cleanup                  | Background asyncio task, cleanup every 5 min               | f65dc05     |
| F-027   | MEDIUM   | Done   | WebSocket ConnectionManager not integrated into lifespan           | Init on startup, graceful disconnect on shutdown           | f65dc05     |
| F-028   | MEDIUM   | Done   | Rate limiter Redis backend uses fixed-bucket approximation         | Sorted set sliding window matching memory backend          | 5164070     |
